fact: May 1 is exactly 121 days into the year - that's 11 squared, making it a perfect square day!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when dandelions explode everywhere like tiny yellow fireworks across lawns and fields.
Why do so many baby animals show up right now? Because May 1 hits the sweet spot when winter's harshness is gone but summer's brutal heat hasn't arrived yet.
May 1 sits smack in the middle of Taurus season, when the zodiac's most stubborn bull is in charge of the sky.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the beginning of winter - while kids up north are picking flowers, kids down south are bundling up!
Tree leaves in northern regions are that perfect bright green color right now because they're brand new and haven't been beaten up by summer sun yet.
The temperature difference between day and night gets really dramatic around May 1 in many places - you might need shorts at lunch and a jacket at bedtime.
Bees go absolutely bonkers around this time because there are suddenly flowers everywhere, making May 1 one of the buzziest days of the year.
May 1 is exactly halfway between the spring equinox and summer solstice - it's like nature's perfect middle point between cold and hot.
In northern lakes and ponds, the water temperature finally gets warm enough that fish start getting super active and hungry after their sleepy winter.
